examples.title: Basic chmod Commands
examples.description: Common chmod commands for modifying file permissions
examples.code:
# Symbolic mode examples
chmod u+rw file.txt # Add read+write for owner
chmod g+r file.txt # Add read for group
chmod o-rwx file.txt # Remove all for others
chmod a+x script.sh # Add execute for all (owner, group, others)
chmod u=rwx,g=rx,o=r file # Set specific permissions

# Numeric mode examples
chmod 644 file.txt # Owner: rw-, Group: r--, Others: r--
chmod 755 script.sh # Owner: rwx, Group: r-x, Others: r-x
chmod 600 private.key # Owner: rw-, Others: ---
chmod 700 private_dir # Owner: rwx, Others: ---

# Special permissions
chmod 4755 program # SetUID + rwxr-xr-x
chmod 2755 shared_dir # SetGID + rwxr-xr-x
chmod 1775 tmp_dir # Sticky bit + rwxr-xr-x

# Recursive
chmod -R 755 /path/to/dir # Change all files and subdirectories
examples.explanation: This demonstrates various chmod commands using both symbolic and numeric modes, plus special permissions and recursive operations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for recursive, O(1) for single file

examples.title: umask Commands
examples.description: How to view and set umask values
examples.code:
# View current umask
umask

# Set umask temporarily (for current session)
umask 022

# Common umask settings
umask 022 # Files: 644, Dirs: 755 (default)
umask 077 # Files: 600, Dirs: 700 (maximum privacy)
umask 002 # Files: 664, Dirs: 775 (group sharing)
umask 027 # Files: 640, Dirs: 750 (restricted group)

# Make permanent in ~/.bashrc
echo "umask 022" >> ~/.bashrc

# Create files and check their permissions
touch newfile.txt
mkdir newdir
ls -l newfile.txt newdir
examples.explanation: Shows how to view and change umask, and how it affects default permissions for newly created files and directories.

examples.title: Permission Checking and Modification Script
examples.description: A script to check and set appropriate permissions for a project directory
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash

# Script to set up secure project permissions

PROJECT_DIR="/var/www/html/myproject"
WEB_USER="www-data"
GROUP_NAME="developers"

echo "Setting up permissions for $PROJECT_DIR..."

# Set directory permissions (rwxr-x---)
chmod 750 "$PROJECT_DIR"
echo "Directory permissions set to 750"

# Set file permissions (rw-r----)
find "$PROJECT_DIR" -type f -exec chmod 640 {} \;
echo "File permissions set to 640"

# Make scripts executable
find "$PROJECT_DIR" -name "*.sh" -exec chmod 750 {} \;
echo "Script permissions updated"

# Set special permission on upload directory (sticky bit)
chmod 1777 "$PROJECT_DIR/uploads"
echo "Upload directory set with sticky bit"

# Verify permissions
ls -laR "$PROJECT_DIR" | head -20

echo "Permission setup complete!"
examples.explanation: This script demonstrates practical permission management for a web project, including directories, files, scripts, and special sticky bit for upload directories.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of files
